title: Raspberry & Elderflower Mille-Feuille
summary: If the thought of making mille-feuille scares you, look no further. This here is an easy alternative to this classic French dessert. C'est bon.

method.description: Take your puff pastry out of the fridge 25 mins before starting. Unroll the pastry sheet and cut into 12 equal pieces, prick with a fork, then transfer to a lined baking tray, making sure there is space in between each one. Dust with 4 tbsp of icing sugar, then top with another piece of baking paper. Weigh down with a baking tray and cook for 20 min. Until golden and crisp.
Whip the cream into stiff peaks, then fold through the custard and vanilla. Get the raspberries into a bowl with the elderflower cordial, sieve in the remaining icing sugar and mix.

Transfer the cream mixture into a piping bag and pipe onto 1 piece of the pastry, give it a little smoosh with a spoon to make a well, then fill with some of the raspberries. Repeat until you have 3 layers of pastry then dust with icing sugar.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
